g.data-narration: Welcome to our lecture on Enumerations in Java. Learn about type-safe constants with fields, methods, and interfaces.
An enumeration is a special data type defining fixed constant objects. It provides compile-time type safety for related values.
g.text: A special data type that defines a fixed set of constant objects.
Provides compile-time type checking vs int/String constants.

g.data-narration: Before enums, developers used int constants which lacked type safety. Enums prevent invalid values and make code self-documenting.
g.text: No type safety - can pass any int

g.data-narration: Every enum implicitly extends java.lang.Enum. Constants are objects of the enum class, automatically generated by the compiler.
Enums can have fields to store data per constant. Constructors are always private - the JVM ensures only defined constants exist.
Java provides automatic methods: values() returns all constants, ordinal() gives position, valueOf() finds by name, name() returns constant name.
Enums work perfectly with switch statements. Use constant name only (RED), not the type prefix (TrafficLight.RED).
Enums can implement interfaces, allowing different behavior per constant. Each constant provides its own implementation of abstract methods.

text.tspan: interface Operation { double apply(double a, double b); }
PLUS("+") { public double apply(double a, double b) { return a+b; } },
MINUS("-") { public double apply(double a, double b) { return a-b; } };

g.data-narration: EnumSet is a highly efficient Set using bit vectors internally. Much faster than HashSet for enum keys. Not thread-safe by default.
EnumMap is a Map optimized for enum keys. Uses ordinal-based array internally for O(1) performance. Not thread-safe.
Here's a practical example: Direction enum with x,y coordinates and an opposite() method returning the opposite direction.

text.tspan: Direction(int dx, int dy) { this.dx=dx; this.dy=dy; }

g.data-narration: Enums can have abstract methods forcing each constant to provide its implementation. Perfect for calculator-style operations.

text.tspan: ADD { double apply(double a,double b){return a+b;} },
MULTIPLY { double apply(double a,double b){return a*b;} };
abstract double apply(double a, double b);

g.data-narration: Avoid these exam mistakes: never store ordinal() in databases, handle valueOf() exceptions, remember collections aren't thread-safe.
In summary: Enums are type-safe constants that can have fields, methods, and implement interfaces. Use EnumSet and EnumMap for efficiency.
g.text: • Private constructors - cannot use new operator
• Built-in methods: values(), ordinal(), valueOf(), name()
• Switch support - use constant name without type prefix
• Can implement interfaces but not extend classes
• Fields & methods for associated data per constant
